name: Thumb_F16_ConditionalBranch
comment: 1101 cccc oooooooo — B<cond> with 8-bit signed offset (scaled by 2). cond=1111 is reserved for SWI (F17, must be matched first by priority order).

instructions.comment: Single mnemonic 'B' with runtime cond evaluation; ARM disassemblers spell as BEQ/BNE/etc by cond field. writes_pc=always (matches ARM Bcc) — when cond passes the instruction always writes PC; the conditional-execution gate (cond field) is orthogonal to the writes_pc classification, which describes the instruction's semantic effect when its body executes. Prior 'conditional_via_rd' was wrong (that's reserved for ALU ops whose Rd may be PC) and caused block-JIT to span past Thumb branches, breaking BIOS LLE polling loops.
